Quickstart Guide: zzo2f (Typst Backend)

Ronald G. Thomas

Source: vignettes/quickstart_zzo2f.Rmd
quickstart_zzo2f.Rmd

This quickstart covers zzo2f(), the Typst-based alternative to o2f(). Where o2f() requires a full LaTeX distribution (pdflatex + pdfcrop + ImageMagick), zzo2f() needs only two lightweight dependencies: the tinytable R package and the Typst CLI (~40 MB binary). Typst auto-sizes pages, so no cropping step is needed.

Both functions share the same theme system. The key difference is the compilation backend: LaTeX vs Typst.

Installation 

# R package
install.packages("tinytable")

# Typst CLI
# macOS:   brew install typst
# Linux:   curl -fsSL https://typst.community | sh
# Windows: winget install --id Typst.Typst
library(zzobj2fig)
#> zzobj2fig 0.3.0 - LaTeX table generation for R

Verify that both dependencies are available:

check_typst_deps()

Basic Usage

Convert a data frame to a table image:

zzo2f(
  mtcars[1:6, 1:4],
  filename = "basic_table",
  sub_dir = output_dir,
  format = "png"
)

By default, zzo2f() produces a PDF. Here we use format = "png" for display. The output is a single file with no intermediate artifacts (no .tex, no _full.pdf, no cropping step).

Output Formats

zzo2f() supports three output formats via the format argument:

zzo2f(mtcars[1:6, 1:4],
      filename = "fmt_pdf",
      sub_dir = output_dir,
      format = "pdf")
zzo2f(mtcars[1:6, 1:4],
      filename = "fmt_png",
      sub_dir = output_dir,
      format = "png",
      dpi = 300)
zzo2f(mtcars[1:6, 1:4],
      filename = "fmt_svg",
      sub_dir = output_dir,
      format = "svg")

PNG output respects the dpi argument (default 300). SVG output produces resolution-independent vector graphics suitable for web documents.

Journal Themes

zzo2f() reuses the same theme system as o2f(). All built-in themes translate automatically to Typst styling:

zzo2f(mtcars[1:6, 1:4],
      theme = "nejm",
      filename = "nejm_table",
      sub_dir = output_dir,
      format = "png")
zzo2f(mtcars[1:6, 1:4],
      theme = "apa",
      filename = "apa_table",
      sub_dir = output_dir,
      format = "png")

Available themes: "nejm", "apa", "nature", "lancet", "minimal".

The global theme set via o2f_theme_set() is respected by zzo2f() as well:

o2f_theme_set("nejm")
zzo2f(mtcars[1:6, 1:4], filename = "auto_themed")

Captions and Footnotes 

fn <- o2f_footnote(
  general = "Data from Motor Trend US magazine (1974).",
  symbol = c("p < 0.05", "p < 0.01")
)

zzo2f(mtcars[1:6, 1:4],
      caption = "Motor Trend Car Road Tests",
      footnote = fn,
      filename = "captioned_table",
      sub_dir = output_dir,
      format = "png")

Footnotes from o2f_footnote() are translated to plain-text notes below the table. General, numbered, alphabetic, and symbol notation styles are all supported.

Spanning Headers

Group columns under spanning headers using o2f_header_above():

hdr <- o2f_header_above(
  " " = 1,
  "Performance" = 2,
  "Engine" = 1
)

zzo2f(mtcars[1:6, 1:4],
      header_above = hdr,
      filename = "header_table",
      sub_dir = output_dir,
      format = "png")

Column Alignment

Alignment is auto-detected (numeric columns right-aligned, others left-aligned) or set explicitly:

zzo2f(iris[1:6, ],
      align = c("r", "r", "r", "r", "l"),
      filename = "aligned_table",
      sub_dir = output_dir,
      format = "png")

A single character applies to all columns:

zzo2f(mtcars[1:6, 1:4], align = "c")

Row Stripe Color Override

Override the theme’s stripe color with scolor:

zzo2f(mtcars[1:6, 1:4],
      scolor = "blue!15",
      filename = "blue_stripes",
      sub_dir = output_dir,
      format = "png")

LaTeX color syntax ("blue!15", "gray!5") is automatically translated to hex. Hex values ("#E6E6FF") pass through unchanged.

Working with Statistical Objects

zzo2f() supports S3 dispatch for common statistical objects (lm, glm, anova, aov, htest), automatically extracting, rounding, and formatting coefficients:

model <- lm(mpg ~ cyl + hp + wt, data = mtcars)

zzo2f(model,
      caption = "Linear Model Coefficients",
      theme = "nejm",
      filename = "lm_typst",
      sub_dir = output_dir,
      format = "png")

For full control over column selection, you can also use the include parameter or pass a pre-formatted data frame directly.

Model Comparison

Compare multiple models side-by-side with zzo2f_regression():

m1 <- lm(mpg ~ cyl, data = mtcars)
m2 <- lm(mpg ~ cyl + hp, data = mtcars)
m3 <- lm(mpg ~ cyl + hp + wt, data = mtcars)

zzo2f_regression(
  Model1 = m1, Model2 = m2, Model3 = m3,
  stars = TRUE,
  filename = "model_comparison",
  sub_dir = output_dir,
  format = "png"
)

A separator line is automatically inserted between coefficient rows and summary statistics (N, R-squared, Adj. R-squared).

Helper Functions

The zzo2f() pipeline supports a family of zzo2f_* helpers that parallel the LaTeX-side o2f_* helpers. These return small spec objects or Typst source fragments. The sections below show representative usage.

Cell-level formatting

Formatting specs are consumed by zzo2f() via its formats parameter, which accepts a single spec or a list of specs.

library(zzobj2fig)

df <- mtcars[1:4, 1:4]

zzo2f(
  df,
  filename = 'styled_demo',
  formats = list(
    zzo2f_bold_col('mpg'),
    zzo2f_italic_col('hp'),
    zzo2f_color_row(c(1, 3), background = 'gray!10'),
    zzo2f_highlight(
      function(x) as.numeric(x) > 20,
      background = 'yellow!30'
    ),
    zzo2f_decimal(c('mpg', 'disp'), digits = 1)
  )
)

zzo2f_format() is the general constructor; the other five are thin wrappers for common cases.

Collapsed rows and spanning headers

zzo2f_collapse_rows() visually merges consecutive identical values in the specified columns; zzo2f_header_above() delegates to o2f_header_above() and is consumed via header_above.

df <- data.frame(
  Group    = rep(c('A', 'B'), each = 3),
  Subgroup = rep(c('x', 'y', 'z'), 2),
  Value    = 1:6
)

zzo2f(
  df,
  filename = 'collapsed',
  collapse_rows = zzo2f_collapse_rows('Group'),
  header_above = zzo2f_header_above(
    ' ' = 1, 'Identifier' = 2
  )
)

Footnotes, marks, and cross-references

zzo2f_footnote() is an alias for o2f_footnote(); the spec is backend-agnostic. zzo2f_mark() inserts Typst superscript markers; zzo2f_ref() returns a Typst cross-reference string (@label).

df <- mtcars[1:3, 1:3]
df$mpg[1] <- zzo2f_mark(as.character(df$mpg[1]), 1, 'symbol')

zzo2f(
  df,
  filename = 'fn_demo',
  footnote = zzo2f_footnote(
    general = 'Source: mtcars (Motor Trend, 1974).',
    symbol = c('Leading observation.')
  )
)

ref_str <- zzo2f_ref('tab-fn-demo', cat = FALSE)

Batch rendering 

tables <- list(
  cars   = mtcars[1:5, 1:3],
  irises = iris[1:5,   1:3]
)
zzo2f_batch(tables, sub_dir = 'figures', theme = 'nejm')

# Per-table overrides
specs <- list(
  zzo2f_batch_spec(mtcars[1:3, 1:3], 'mt_small'),
  zzo2f_batch_spec(iris[1:3,   1:3], 'ir_small',
                   theme = 'apa')
)
zzo2f_batch_advanced(specs, sub_dir = 'figures')

Fitted models

zzo2f_tidy() routes a broom::tidy() output through zzo2f(); zzo2f_coef() is an inline wrapper for use in R Markdown; zzo2f_rms_compare() builds a side-by-side comparison of rms models.

m <- lm(mpg ~ cyl + hp, data = mtcars)
zzo2f_tidy(m, filename = 'lm_tidy', digits = 2)
zzo2f_coef(m, caption = 'Model coefficients.')

Embedding a rendered table in a Typst document

The zzo2f_include*() helpers emit Typst source for embedding a previously rendered PDF into a host .typ document. They are invoked for their side effect (print-and-return) in R Markdown chunks, or captured via cat = FALSE.

zzo2f(mtcars[1:3, 1:3], filename = 'inner')

zzo2f_include(
  'inner',
  caption = 'Demo embedded table.',
  label = 'tab-inner',
  cat = FALSE
)
#> "#align(center)[#figure(image(\"inner.pdf\", width: 100%), ..."

# Side-by-side
zzo2f_include_sidebyside(
  'fig/a', 'fig/b',
  main_caption = 'Comparison',
  cat = FALSE
)

Note that Typst refuses paths outside its project root, so place the host .typ file in the same directory as (or above) the referenced PDFs.

Preamble directives

Programmatic emitters for .typ preamble lines:

preamble <- c(
  zzo2f_page(paper = 'a4',
             margin = list(x = '2cm', y = '2cm')),
  zzo2f_textlang('en'),
  zzo2f_font(main_font = 'Libertinus Serif')
)

These are intentionally thin; most users will write preamble directives directly in their .typ source.

Advanced: source-level post-processing

For users who want to transform the generated Typst source before compilation, pass compile = FALSE to zzo2f():

src <- zzo2f(
  mtcars[1:3, 1:3],
  filename = 'raw_demo',
  compile = FALSE
)
# src is a character vector of .typ lines with attributes
# recording filename / sub_dir / format.

src <- c('// Added by hand', src)
zzo2f_compile(src, filename = 'raw_demo')

Quick Reference

zzo2f() Parameters

Parameter Description Default
filename Base name (no extension) deparsed input
sub_dir Output directory "analysis/figures"
format "pdf", "png", or "svg" "pdf"
dpi PNG resolution 300
caption Table caption NULL
align Column alignment (l/c/r) NULL (auto)
theme Theme name or object NULL
scolor Row stripe color override NULL
footnote o2f_footnote() object NULL
header_above o2f_header_above() object NULL
verbose Print progress messages FALSE

New parameters added by the helper set

Parameter Description
formats A zzo2f_format spec or list of specs.
collapse_rows A zzo2f_collapse_rows() spec.
compile If FALSE, return Typst source instead of compiling.

Parameters from o2f() still without a Typst equivalent

  • extra_packages, document_class: preamble is built by zzo2f_page() / zzo2f_font() / zzo2f_textlang() or by the user directly.
  • caption_short: Typst #figure has no short-caption concept.
  • crop, crop_margin: Typst auto-sizes the page; cropping is unnecessary.
  • longtable: multi-page tables are not yet wired through zzo2f() although Typst itself supports them natively.

Choosing Between o2f() and zzo2f()

o2f() zzo2f()
Backend LaTeX (kableExtra) Typst (tinytable)
Dependencies pdflatex + pdfcrop + ImageMagick Typst CLI (~40 MB)
Output formats PDF (+ PNG/SVG via convert) PDF, PNG, SVG natively
S3 dispatch Yes (lm, glm, rms, …) Yes (lm, glm, anova, htest)
Cropping Required (pdfcrop) Not needed (auto-sized)
Decimal alignment siunitx S columns zzo2f_siunitx() via Typst metro (partial)
Multi-page tables longtable Not yet wired through zzo2f()
Collapse rows multirow zzo2f_collapse_rows() (cell-emptying)
Cell formatting o2f_format() family zzo2f_format() family
Figure inclusion o2f_include*() family zzo2f_include*() family
Batch rendering o2f_batch*() family zzo2f_batch*() family
Stat helpers o2f_tidy, o2f_coef, o2f_rms_compare zzo2f_tidy, zzo2f_coef, zzo2f_rms_compare

Use o2f() when you need multi-page tables or the full siunitx option set. Use zzo2f() when you want fast compilation with minimal system dependencies or native PNG/SVG output without ImageMagick. The helper APIs are now close to symmetric across the two backends.

System Requirements

  • R >= 4.1 (for native pipe |>)
  • tinytable R package
  • Typst CLI (>= 0.11)

Check your setup:

check_typst_deps()

On this page
